---
title: "Helpful Technical Documentation"
author: Greg Brooks
date: 10-4-2025
categories: [website-maintenance]
draft: true
execute: 
    freeze: true
---

This list is mostly for Greg as he maintains this website.

# Useful snippets
- {{< var ai_disclaimer >}}
- [alt text](../../images/soilsample_sketch.jpg){width=50%}
- use *** instead of triple hyphen to avoid rendering errors (occasional, but annoying)
- Example of how to make links open in a new window: [Apra International Salary Survey](https://aprahome.org/Resources/Salary-Survey){target="_blank"}
- internal links: 

# Quarto Tips
## Extensions

### DownloadThis
- DownloadThis button example: {{< downloadthis file.png dname=coolfile label="Download me!" >}}
- [DownloadThis website](https://shafayetshafee.github.io/downloadthis/example.html)


## Quarto guides
- [Practical Quarto](https://quarto-tdg.org/websites.html)
- [Quarto presentations](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/presenting.html)

## Website Maintenance
- Use the [drafts feature](https://quarto.org/docs/websites/website-drafts.html) to preview and plan content
- Learn how to use the profiles feature
- Prevent all unnecessary folders and files from rendering to the website (how?)
- learn [incremental render](https://quarto.org/docs/projects/code-execution.html)

Yes, you can preview `.qmd` files inside an excluded folder like `scripts/` **without moving them**, but it requires a bit of manual action because Quarto won’t render them automatically during your site build.

Here’s how to do it:

---

## How to preview `.qmd` files in excluded folders without moving them

### 1. Render them manually, one at a time

From your project root, run:

```bash
quarto render scripts/your_file.qmd
```

* This renders just that file (ignores your site’s `render` exclusion)
* Output goes to `_output` folder by default (not your website’s `docs/`)
* You can open the generated HTML in your browser to preview

---

### 2. Use the `quarto preview` command for that file

```bash
quarto preview scripts/your_file.qmd
```

* Opens a local preview server with live reload for that file
* Handy for iterative editing and testing

---

### 3. Use VS Code Quarto extension to preview

If you have the Quarto extension in VS Code:

* Open `scripts/your_file.qmd`
* Use **“Preview”** button in editor (usually top right or right-click menu)
* This previews the current file regardless of site config

---

### 4. Temporarily disable the exclusion (if you want full site preview)

Remove or comment out the `render: - "!scripts/**"` line

* Run `quarto render` to include those files
* Preview site with those pages included
* Re-enable exclusion when done

---

## Summary

| Method                        | Pros                     | Cons                            |
| ----------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------- |
| `quarto render file.qmd`      | Fast, manual render      | Manual, one file at a time      |
| `quarto preview file.qmd`     | Live reload, interactive | One file only                   |
| VS Code Quarto preview        | Integrated, easy         | Requires VS Code & extension    |
| Disable exclusion temporarily | Full site preview        | Changes site config temporarily |

---
## Database Architecture
- [RSQLite](https://ecorepsci.github.io/reproducible-science/rsqlite.html#a-note-on-reproducibility)
- [SQLite mobile editing](https://sqliteonline.com/)
- [Baserow docs](https://baserow.io/user-docs/database-api)

## VS-Code
- [Markdown All-in-One commands](https://markdown-all-in-one.github.io/docs/guide/#features)

## Quarto Extensions
- [Quarto Extensions and also great example of a quarto website](https://m.canouil.dev/quarto-extensions/)
- [embedio](https://github.com/coatless-quarto/embedio/blob/main/README.md) for presentations embedded in a Quarto website
- [editable reveal js](https://emilhvitfeldt.github.io/quarto-revealjs-editable/) lets you do some basic editing within revealjs presentations themselves, and then save it back to your QMD, which makes for a nice workflow similar to working in powerpoint

### Visualization Extensions
- [Diagrams](https://github.com/pandoc-ext/diagram)
- [Svelte components](https://quarto-svelte.jamesgoldie.dev/#installation)

### Article Interactivity 
- [Lightboxes](https://github.com/quarto-ext/lightbox)
- [expanding "nutshell" notes so people don't have to switch tabs](https://github.com/schochastics/quarto-nutshell)

### Embedding
- [social media embeds, easy to embed YouTube](https://quarto-social-embeds.sellorm.com/)
- [PDF embedding](https://jmgirard.github.io/embedpdf/example.html)

### User Experience
- [downloading embedded data or images](https://shafayetshafee.github.io/downloadthis/example.html)
- [list of links at end of page](https://github.com/coatless-quarto/linkate)
- [Quarto badges](https://m.canouil.dev/quarto-badge/), lets you make fun stuff like {{< badge toolbadge this! >}}

## Example Quarto Sites
- [good example of webr and download capabilities](https://insightsengineering.github.io/tlg-catalog/stable/)
